children.heading: Export of certain goods or technology
children.label: a Export of certain goods or technology
children.text: A validated license shall be required for the export to North Korea of any goods or technology otherwise covered under section 4605(j) of title 50. No defense exports may be approved for the Government of North Korea.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Transactions in defense articles or defense services
children.label: b Transactions in defense articles or defense services

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The President shall withhold assistance under the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ( et seq.) to the government of any country that provides to or receives from the Government of North Korea a defense article or defense service, as such terms are defined in , if the President determines that a significant type or amount of such article or service has been so provided or received.22 U.S.C. 2151section 2794 of this title

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: 2 Applicability
children.text: The prohibition under paragraph (1) with respect to a government shall terminate on the date that is 5 years after the date on which the prohibition under paragraph (1) is applied to that government.
determines that such waiver is in the national interest of the United States; and

children.label: A
children.text: the steps that the relevant agencies are taking to curtail the trade described in subsection (b)(1); and

children.label: B
children.text: why such waiver is in the national interest of the United States.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: d Exception
children.text: The prohibitions under this section shall not apply to the provision of assistance for human rights, democracy, rule of law, maternal and child health, disease prevention and response, or humanitarian purposes.

children.heading: Report on arms trafficking involving North Korea
children.label: e Report on arms trafficking involving North Korea

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , and annually thereafter for 5 years, the Secretary of State shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees a report that specifically describes the compliance of foreign countries and other foreign jurisdictions with the requirement to curtail the trade described in subsection (b)(1).August 2, 2017

children.heading: Form
children.label: 2 Form
children.text: The report required under paragraph (1) shall be submitted in unclassified form but may contain a classified annex.
